description: Bootstrap scripts prepare a clean machine to build FastCode.
steps: Run init script on target OS (init.ps1 or init.sh).
Confirm prerequisite checks pass or fail with clear actionable error.
Confirm dependency fetch/build steps execute and produce success output.
evidence: 2026-02-14: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ./init.ps1 exited 0.
2026-02-14: sh ./init.sh exited 0.

description: CLI entrypoint starts and accepts runtime mode argument.
steps: Run cargo run -- --mode edit.
Observe process startup output includes selected mode.
Exit process without crash and return code 0.
evidence: 2026-02-14: cargo run -- --mode edit printed selected mode and exited 0.

description: Runtime mode permissions are enforceable for plan/edit/auto.
steps: Create unit tests for mode capability matrix.
Run cargo test and verify all permission assertions pass.
Record test output snippet in evidence.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added src/modes/runtime_mode.rs with plan/edit/auto capability matrix (can_write/can_execute/requires_approval) and parsing.
2026-02-14: cargo test passed with test_runtime_mode_permissions and runtime mode parse/round-trip tests (5 passed, 0 failed).
2026-02-14: cargo run -- --mode edit|plan|auto exited 0; cargo run -- --mode invalid failed with explicit mode validation error.

description: Policy validator blocks denied shell patterns and protects paths.
steps: Load sample allow/deny/protected configuration.
Execute validation for allowed and denied commands.
Verify denied cases produce explicit policy errors.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added policy module in src/policy/validator.rs with PolicyConfig + PolicyValidator for allow/deny command checks and protected path checks.
2026-02-14: Added policy unit tests for JSON config loading, allowed command, denied command explicit errors, and protected path blocking.
2026-02-14: cargo test passed including policy::validator tests (11 passed, 0 failed).

description: Approval manager persists allow_once/allow_session/allow_global decisions.
steps: Grant each approval type for sample command prefix.
Reload persisted approvals from disk.
Verify lookup behavior matches approval scope semantics.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added approvals module at src/approvals/manager.rs with ApprovalManager persistence to JSON and scope-aware approvals (allow_once/allow_session/allow_global).
2026-02-14: Added unit tests covering add -> save -> reload and scope lookup semantics, including allow_once consumption after first authorization.
2026-02-14: cargo test passed with approvals::manager tests and full suite (14 passed, 0 failed).

description: Session manager can create, list, load, and switch sessions.
steps: Create two sessions and persist them.
List sessions and verify metadata is present.
Load a selected session and verify active context switches correctly.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added session module at src/session/manager.rs + src/session/mod.rs with SessionManager create/list/load/switch and JSON persistence under configurable sessions directory.
2026-02-14: Added session manager unit tests for create->persist->list metadata and load/switch active session flows, plus invalid id rejection.
2026-02-14: cargo test passed full suite including session::manager tests (17 passed, 0 failed).

description: Tool registry executes built-in shell/file/git tools through a common interface.
steps: Register tool definitions in registry.
Invoke each tool with valid and invalid arguments.
Verify normalized success/error result envelopes.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added tools module (`src/tools/registry.rs`, `src/tools/builtin.rs`, `src/tools/mod.rs`) with Tool trait, ToolRegistry, built-in shell/file/git tools, and normalized ToolResultEnvelope (`status`, `output`, `error_code`, `error_message`).

2026-02-14: Added unit test `tools::builtin::tests::registry_executes_builtin_tools_with_normalized_envelopes` covering registry definition registration, success + invalid arguments for shell/file/git tools, and unregistered tool handling.
2026-02-14: cargo test passed full suite including tool registry workflow (18 passed, 0 failed).

description: TUI renders status bar, message area, and input box and handles key events.
steps: Launch TUI app in terminal.
Type input, submit, and scroll messages with keyboard.
Verify mode indicator and loading/status UI update correctly.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added TUI module `src/tui/mod.rs` with status bar + message area + input box rendering, and key handling for type/backspace/enter/up/down/q.
2026-02-14: Added TUI interaction tests (input submit, scroll, status transition, render-region assertions, scripted key parsing); cargo test passed (22 passed, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Verified interactive workflow via real TUI loop using scripted key events: `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,e,l,l,o,Enter,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui` (render showed mode indicator, processing status, message updates, and clean quit).

description: Streaming agent output is delivered incrementally to UI without blocking input loop.
steps: Start a chat request that streams multi-part output.
Observe incremental rendering in UI.
Verify completion event closes stream cleanly.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Upgraded `src/tui/mod.rs` to stream assistant output incrementally via chunked tick-based rendering (`STREAM_CHUNK_INTERVAL_MS`, `chunk_text`, `StreamState`) instead of single-shot delayed append.

2026-02-14: Added TUI tests for streaming lifecycle and non-blocking input during processing (`processing_transitions_back_to_idle_and_appends_assistant_reply`, `streaming_does_not_block_user_typing_during_processing`) and ran `cargo test` (24 passed, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Verified real scripted UI flow with incremental render and clean completion: `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,e,l,l,o,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,sleep80,sleep80,sleep80,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui` (terminal output showed chunk-by-chunk assistant text growth and status returning `processing -> idle`).

description: Audit logger writes tool invocation records with timestamp, mode, and result.
steps: Trigger at least two tool calls in different modes.
Open audit log file and inspect JSONL entries.
Verify required fields are present and parseable.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added audit logger module (`src/audit/logger.rs`) with JSONL append behavior and required fields (`timestamp_unix_ms`, `mode`, `tool`, `result`, `error_code`, `error_message`).
2026-02-14: Extended `ToolRegistry` with `execute_with_audit` in `src/tools/registry.rs` so tool invocations are logged with runtime mode and normalized success/error result.

2026-02-14: Added end-to-end tests `audit::logger::tests::appends_parseable_jsonl_records_with_required_fields` and `tools::builtin::tests::writes_audit_records_for_tool_invocations_across_modes`; `cargo test` passed (26 passed, 0 failed).

description: MCP server configuration can be loaded and listed via CLI with validation errors.
steps: Provide MCP JSON config with at least two servers.
Run CLI command to list MCP servers from config.
Verify missing-config and invalid-config paths return actionable errors.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added MCP config module (`src/mcp/config.rs`) with JSON loading, schema validation (non-empty server list/name/command, duplicate-name rejection), and deterministic server-name listing.

2026-02-14: Extended CLI in `src/main.rs` with `--mcp-config` and `--list-mcp-servers`; successful real run: `cargo run -- --mode edit --list-mcp-servers --mcp-config ./docs/mcp-servers.example.json` printed `loaded 2 MCP server(s): filesystem, github`.

2026-02-14: Verified actionable error path: `cargo run -- --mode edit --list-mcp-servers` exited 1 with `--list-mcp-servers requires --mcp-config <path-to-json-config>`; unit tests added for valid load/sort and invalid duplicate/empty command cases (`cargo test`: 34 passed, 0 failed).

description: MCP process manager can spawn configured servers, report health, and shut them down cleanly.
steps: Start at least one MCP server process from validated config.
Check server health state while running and after process exit.
Shut down managed process(es) and verify no running handles remain.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added MCP lifecycle manager in `src/mcp/lifecycle.rs` with process spawn (`start_server`/`start_all`), runtime health checks (`health` => running/exited), and graceful teardown (`shutdown_server`/`shutdown_all`).

2026-02-14: Added lifecycle tests for full workflow and exit-state handling: `starts_checks_health_and_shuts_down_server`, `reports_exited_server_health_state`, `starts_and_stops_all_servers_from_config`; verified with `cargo test mcp::lifecycle` (3 passed, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Regression checks passed after MCP lifecycle integration: `cargo test` (37 passed, 0 failed), `cargo run -- --mode edit`, and scripted real TUI flow `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,e,l,l,o,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui`.

description: Repository handoff artifacts remain auditable across sessions.
steps: Start a fresh coding session with no implicit context.
Reconstruct state using app_spec, feature_list, README, and progress log.
Confirm next priority task can be selected without ambiguity.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Fresh coding session reconstructed full project context by reading authoritative artifacts in order: app_spec.txt, feature_list.json, README.md, claude-progress.txt, plus git history (`git log --oneline -20`).
2026-02-14: Verified unambiguous next-task selection from checklist state via scripted count/sort (`pending_count=5`; highest pending is `NF-001` with priority `P0`, followed by `F-010` `P1`).

2026-02-14: Regression guardrails validated before status update: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ./init.ps1`, `cargo run -- --mode edit`, and scripted real TUI interaction `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,e,l,l,o,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,sleep80,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui` all exited 0.

description: Cold start build time is captured and tracked for optimization.
steps: Clear target directory.
Run timed cargo build.
Store timing evidence and compare against previous baseline.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added reusable cold-start timing script `scripts/measure-cold-start.ps1` that clears `target/`, times `cargo build`, and appends JSONL records to `docs/cold-start-baseline.jsonl` with rust version and elapsed seconds.
2026-02-14: Captured two clean-build baseline samples via `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ./scripts/measure-cold-start.ps1`: first `elapsed_seconds=12.326`, second `elapsed_seconds=12.58`.
2026-02-14: Comparison output recorded by script against previous baseline: `delta_seconds=0.254 (2.06%, slower)`; tracking docs added at `docs/cold-start-baseline.md`.

description: Safety defaults prevent high-risk command execution without policy/approval.
steps: Attempt denied command through tool interface.
Verify command is blocked before execution.
Verify user-facing error explains why and how to resolve.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added `ToolRegistry::execute_with_safety` in `src/tools/registry.rs` to enforce runtime-mode execution rules, policy deny checks, and edit-mode approval requirements before shell execution.

2026-02-14: Added test `tools::builtin::tests::blocks_denied_shell_command_before_execution_with_resolution_error` that sends a real high-risk delete command through the tool interface, confirms `policy_denied` with remediation text (`To proceed...`), and verifies target file still exists (blocked before execution).

2026-02-14: Added test `tools::builtin::tests::blocks_shell_command_without_required_approval_in_edit_mode` that verifies `approval_required` without prior approval and successful execution after granting `allow_once`; validated via `cargo test` and targeted test runs.

description: Terminal UX remains usable across standard desktop terminal sizes.
steps: Run TUI at 80x24 and 120x40 terminal sizes.
Verify no critical content clipping in status/input areas.
Verify navigation and input remain functional after resize.

2026-02-14: Added TUI tests `draw_supports_standard_terminal_sizes` and `resize_keeps_input_and_navigation_functional` plus size parser coverage (`parses_terminal_size_token`); `cargo test` passed full suite (31 passed, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Verified real UI interaction path with keyboard + resize + submit/scroll in terminal loop: `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,e,l,l,o,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,Up,Down,resize120x40,w,o,r,l,d,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui` and `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='t,e,s,t,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,resize80x24,Up,Down,b,a,c,k,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui` (status updated to `size: 120x40` and `size: 80x24`, input/navigation remained functional, exited 0).

description: CLI can execute MCP lifecycle workflow (start/health/shutdown) with explicit success and failure errors.
steps: Run lifecycle check command with a valid MCP config and observe start/health/shutdown output.
Run lifecycle check command without --mcp-config and verify explicit actionable error.
Confirm lifecycle workflow leaves no managed MCP handles after shutdown.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added CLI flag --check-mcp-lifecycle in src/main.rs and integrated MCP lifecycle check flow via run_lifecycle_check in src/mcp/lifecycle.rs.

2026-02-14: Success-path real run with temp config: cargo run -- --mode edit --check-mcp-lifecycle --mcp-config <temp> printed started 1 MCP server(s), health check running, and shutdown complete: stopped 1 server(s).

2026-02-14: Failure-path real run: cargo run -- --mode edit --check-mcp-lifecycle exited 1 with --check-mcp-lifecycle requires --mcp-config <path-to-json-config>; lifecycle report test mcp::lifecycle::tests::lifecycle_check_reports_start_health_and_shutdown_counts plus full cargo test passed (38 passed, 0 failed).

description: MCP lifecycle workflow can write auditable start/health/shutdown JSONL events through the shared audit logger.
steps: Run lifecycle check with a valid MCP config and --audit-log output path.
Inspect generated JSONL records and verify start/health/shutdown events are present per managed server.
Verify mode/result/error fields are parseable and consistent with shared AuditRecord schema.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Added `run_lifecycle_check_with_audit` in `src/mcp/lifecycle.rs` and kept existing `run_lifecycle_check` backward compatible via shared internal execution path.
2026-02-14: Added lifecycle audit test `mcp::lifecycle::tests::lifecycle_check_writes_audit_events_for_start_health_and_shutdown`; `cargo test` passed full suite (39 passed, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Real CLI verification with audit output: `cargo run -- --mode edit --check-mcp-lifecycle --mcp-config target/mcp-lifecycle-test.json --audit-log target/mcp-lifecycle-audit.jsonl` produced lifecycle success output and JSONL events `mcp_lifecycle:start:sleepy`, `mcp_lifecycle:health:sleepy`, `mcp_lifecycle:shutdown:sleepy` with mode `edit`.

description: TUI surfaces MCP runtime diagnostics in status bar and message area when launched with --mcp-config.

Verify status bar renders an MCP health state and message area includes diagnostics summary.

evidence: 2026-02-14: Added `McpDiagnostics` injection path in `src/tui/mod.rs` (`App::new_with_mcp_diagnostics`, `run_app_with_mcp_diagnostics`) and rendered MCP state in status bar (`mcp: ...`) plus diagnostics messages in message area.

2026-02-14: Updated `src/main.rs` to run MCP lifecycle diagnostics before TUI launch when `--mcp-config` is provided; diagnostics now report `ok`, `degraded`, `error`, or `invalid-config` with explicit user-visible messages.

2026-02-14: Verification: `cargo test` passed (40 passed, 0 failed) including new TUI test `injects_mcp_diagnostics_into_status_and_messages`; real UI path `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,i,Enter,sleep80,sleep80,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-config target/mcp-tui-test.json` rendered `mcp: ok 1/1` in status bar while preserving input/submit/scroll flow; malformed/invalid config path rendered `mcp: invalid-config` and explicit parse error message in message area.

2026-02-14: Real command verification: `cargo run -- --mode edit mcp list --mcp-config ./docs/mcp-servers.example.json` (loaded 2 servers), `cargo run -- --mode edit mcp check-lifecycle --mcp-config target/mcp-cli-subcommand-test.json --audit-log target/mcp-cli-subcommand-audit.jsonl` (started/health/shutdown all succeeded), and explicit error paths for ambiguous inputs returned non-zero with clear messages.

description: MCP lifecycle start-failure path reports explicit partial-start cleanup status and writes auditable error events.
steps: Run lifecycle check with one valid server and one invalid command to force startup failure.
Verify CLI error includes failed server name plus partial start/shutdown counts.
Verify audit JSONL contains start success, start error, and shutdown cleanup events.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Hardened `run_lifecycle_check_inner` in `src/mcp/lifecycle.rs` to start servers incrementally, log `mcp_start_failed` audit events, and force shutdown cleanup for already-started servers before returning an error.

2026-02-14: Added test `mcp::lifecycle::tests::lifecycle_check_start_failure_is_auditable_and_cleans_up_started_servers`; `cargo test mcp::lifecycle` passed (6 passed, 0 failed), full `cargo test` passed (41 passed, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Real failure-path verification: `cargo run -- --mode edit mcp check-lifecycle --mcp-config target/mcp-lifecycle-failure-test.json --audit-log target/mcp-lifecycle-failure-audit.jsonl` returned `MCP lifecycle start failed for server 'broken' ... started 1 server(s) before failure and stopped 1`; audit file contained `mcp_lifecycle:start:sleepy` success, `mcp_lifecycle:start:broken` error with `mcp_start_failed`, and `mcp_lifecycle:shutdown:sleepy` success.

description: TUI continuously refreshes MCP diagnostics during runtime when launched with --mcp-config.
steps: Launch TUI with --mcp-config and keep session open long enough for multiple refresh cycles.
Verify MCP status indicator updates with refresh count in status bar.
Verify user input/submit/scroll flow remains functional while refresh is running.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Updated `src/tui/mod.rs` to add periodic MCP diagnostics refresh inside the TUI loop (every 800ms), including status counter display (`mcp: <state> rN`) and refresh summary messages in the message pane.
2026-02-14: Updated `src/main.rs` to pass `--mcp-config` into TUI runtime so refresh stays active after launch-time snapshot.

2026-02-14: Verification: `cargo test` passed (42 passed, 0 failed). Real UI flow: `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,i,Enter,sleep900,sleep900,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-config target/mcp-tui-refresh-test.json` showed status progression `mcp: ok 1/1 r0 -> r1 -> r2 -> r3` while input/submit/scroll remained functional and process exited 0.

description: TUI MCP diagnostics refresh interval is configurable from CLI for operator-controlled runtime overhead.
steps: Launch TUI with --mcp-config and custom --mcp-refresh-ms value.
Verify MCP refresh counter advances according to configured interval while input/submit/scroll remain functional.
Verify invalid refresh interval values fail with explicit actionable CLI error.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Updated `src/main.rs` to add `--mcp-refresh-ms` (u64 range 1..) and pass it to TUI runtime; added CLI tests `parses_positive_mcp_refresh_interval` and `rejects_zero_mcp_refresh_interval`.
2026-02-14: Updated `src/tui/mod.rs` to replace hardcoded MCP refresh interval with runtime-configurable value while keeping default `DEFAULT_MCP_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS=800` when flag is omitted.

2026-02-14: Verification: `cargo test` passed (42 lib + 5 bin tests, 0 failed). Real UI flow `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,i,Enter,sleep260,sleep260,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-config target/mcp-tui-refresh-test.json --mcp-refresh-ms 200` showed rapid status progression (`mcp: ok 1/1 r1 -> r2 -> r3`) while input/submit/scroll stayed functional. Error-path verification `cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-refresh-ms 0` exited non-zero with explicit range error.

description: TUI MCP diagnostics refresh interval can be configured via environment variable for session-level defaults.
steps: Launch TUI with --mcp-config and FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS set (without --mcp-refresh-ms).
Verify MCP refresh counter advances according to environment-provided interval while input/submit/scroll remain functional.
Verify invalid FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS value fails with explicit actionable error.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Updated `src/main.rs` to resolve refresh interval precedence `--mcp-refresh-ms` > `FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS` > default, with explicit validation errors for non-numeric/zero environment values.

2026-02-14: Added binary tests in `src/main.rs` for environment-path resolution and validation (`resolves_mcp_refresh_interval_from_env_when_cli_missing`, `prefers_cli_mcp_refresh_interval_over_env`, `rejects_invalid_env_mcp_refresh_interval`, `rejects_zero_env_mcp_refresh_interval`); `cargo test` passed (42 lib + 9 bin tests, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Real UI verification without CLI override: `$env:FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS='200'; $env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,i,Enter,sleep260,sleep260,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-config target/mcp-tui-refresh-test.json` showed status progression `mcp: ok 1/1 r1 -> r2 -> r3` with input/submit/scroll working; invalid-path check `$env:FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS='bad'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui` exited non-zero with `FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS must be a positive integer (milliseconds)`.

description: TUI MCP diagnostics refresh interval enforces policy-safe bounds across CLI and environment configuration paths.
steps: Run TUI with an in-range --mcp-refresh-ms value and verify runtime behavior remains stable.
Run TUI with out-of-range --mcp-refresh-ms value and verify explicit range error is returned.
Run TUI with out-of-range FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS value and verify explicit range error is returned.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Updated `src/main.rs` to enforce shared MCP refresh bounds (`50..=60000` ms) for both CLI (`--mcp-refresh-ms`) and environment variable (`FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS`) resolution, including explicit validation errors.

2026-02-14: Added binary tests `rejects_too_large_mcp_refresh_interval` and `rejects_out_of_range_env_mcp_refresh_interval`; updated invalid-env assertion text; `cargo test` passed (42 lib + 10 bin tests, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Real UI verification: `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,i,Enter,sleep260,sleep260,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-config ./docs/mcp-servers.example.json --mcp-refresh-ms 200` preserved input/submit/scroll with MCP refresh progression (`mcp: error r1 -> r2 -> r3`). Error paths: `cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-refresh-ms 60001` failed with clap range error (`60001 is not in 50..=60000`), and `$env:FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS='60001'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui` failed with `FASTCODE_MCP_REFRESH_MS must be in range 50..=60000 milliseconds`.

description: TUI suppresses consecutive duplicate MCP refresh diagnostics summaries to keep the message area readable during persistent failures.
steps: Launch TUI with --mcp-config that produces repeated identical refresh diagnostics.
Verify MCP status refresh counter continues to advance in the status bar.
Verify message area does not append refresh 2/3+ duplicate summaries while input/submit/scroll remain functional.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Updated `src/tui/mod.rs` to track `last_mcp_refresh_signature` and skip appending refresh messages when `status_label + summary` matches the immediately previous refresh result; status counter (`rN`) still increments.
2026-02-14: Added test `tui::tests::deduplicates_identical_consecutive_mcp_refresh_messages`; `cargo test` passed full suite (45 lib tests + 10 bin tests, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Real UI verification with repeated failing diagnostics: `cmd /c "set FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT=h,i,Enter,sleep260,sleep260,sleep260,Up,Down,q && cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-config .\\docs\\mcp-servers.example.json --mcp-refresh-ms 200 > target\\f023-tui-dedupe.log 2>&1"`; parsed log evidence in `target/f023-tui-dedupe.clean.log` showed `refresh1` without `refresh2/refresh3`, while status progressed to `errorr4`.

description: TUI MCP refresh deduplication remains correct when summaries change and when diagnostics omit summary text.
steps: Trigger repeated MCP refreshes with identical summary and verify duplicates are suppressed.
Trigger a subsequent MCP refresh with changed summary and verify a new refresh message is appended.
Trigger MCP refresh with empty diagnostics summary and verify a fallback user-visible message is emitted.
evidence: 2026-02-14: Hardened `App::apply_mcp_refresh` in `src/tui/mod.rs` to always derive a summary (with fallback text when `diagnostics.messages` is empty), keeping status updates auditable instead of silently skipping message output.

2026-02-14: Added tests `tui::tests::appends_new_refresh_message_when_summary_changes` and `tui::tests::appends_fallback_refresh_message_when_summary_is_missing`; `cargo test` passed full suite (47 lib tests + 10 bin tests, 0 failed).

2026-02-14: Real UI regression path verified with scripted interaction: `$env:FASTCODE_TUI_SCRIPT='h,i,Enter,sleep260,sleep260,Up,Down,q'; cargo run -- --mode edit --tui --mcp-config ./docs/mcp-servers.example.json --mcp-refresh-ms 200` (status refresh + input/submit/scroll remained functional, exit 0).

description: TUI provides an explicit user action to inspect full untruncated MCP diagnostics details while preserving compact default message rendering.
steps: Launch TUI with --mcp-config at a narrow viewport where MCP diagnostics lines are compacted.

description: Scripted TUI verification logs can be normalized into machine-parsable evidence without ANSI/control-sequence noise.
